Practical guidance for “Community celebration without waste” within “Community · Psychology · Records”. It supports clear responsibilities, verified information, visible records, fair priorities, stop conditions, and a workable handover, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Stop condition: Never mix cleaning chemicals unless the product instructions explicitly permit it and ventilation is adequate. Apply this boundary to “Community celebration without waste” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Community celebration without waste” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Map drinking water, food, sleeping, washing, toilet, animal, waste, and drainage zones so contamination paths do not cross. Record the result under “Community · Psychology · Records” before continuing “Community celebration without waste”.

Create a handwashing point with soap at food and toilet transitions and make it usable for children and people with limited mobility. Recheck this point before closing “Community celebration without waste” for “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Contain faeces, urine, sharps, chemicals, food waste, and animal waste according to their different hazards. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Community celebration without waste” for “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Clean from lower-risk to higher-risk areas with dedicated tools and avoid splashing or aerosolising contaminated material. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Community celebration without waste”.

Inspect water flow, odour, insects, rodents, overflow, supplies, and illness reports daily and correct the source rather than masking it. Include the outcome of “Community celebration without waste” in the handover for “Community · Psychology · Records”.
warnings: Never mix cleaning chemicals unless the product instructions explicitly permit it and ventilation is adequate. Apply this boundary to “Community celebration without waste” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Keep children, food, drinking water, wounds, and animals away from waste, sewage, sharps, and cleaning chemicals. Apply this boundary to “Community celebration without waste” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Apply this boundary to “Community celebration without waste” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Stop and reassess “Community celebration without waste” when several people develop similar gastrointestinal, skin, eye, or respiratory symptoms. Apply this boundary to “Community celebration without waste” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

This card is not authorization to mix cleaning chemicals, dry-sweep contaminated dust, handle sharps by hand, or place toilets near a drinking-water source.
